message:
fix(serve): make Content-Type check case-insensitive per RFC 2616

The isJSONContent function was doing case-sensitive comparison of
Content-Type headers, which could miss valid JSON responses with
headers like "Application/JSON" or "APPLICATION/json".

RFC 2616 Section 3.7 specifies that media type and subtype are
case-insensitive, so "application/json" == "Application/JSON".

Changes:
- Add strings.ToLower() before Content-Type comparison
- Add doc comment referencing RFC 2616

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
